title: Brand Matters: How FIGS Is Turning Purpose into Profit in Healthcare Apparel
text: FIGS has shifted from discount-driven model to premium, purpose-focused approach, deliberately reducing promotional activity while investing in brand equity and community building. Key strategic pillars: Community-driven positioning reinforcing professional identity for healthcare workers beyond functional uniforms. Global expansion with international sales growing 31% YoY, representing 15% of revenue, with planned entry into Japan and South Korea. Omnichannel integration through Community Hub stores creating offline touchpoints bridging digital and retail. B2B channel development through TEAMS program targeting hospitals and healthcare institutions. FIGS demonstrates purpose translating to business outcomes with $555.6M in 2024 revenue (+2% YoY), domestic market showing stagnation while international operations fuel growth, and margin protection through reduced discounting. Strategic board addition of Jerry Jao (AI marketing founder) signals investment in data-driven decision-making supporting expansion. Central tension: proving upper-funnel brand investments drive measurable sales in complex multi-channel ecosystem spanning DTC, B2B, and international markets.
